intro: Bolt.new and Iterative.ai overlap on scores and pricing posture, so the decision comes down to which problem each was actually built for. Bolt.new is browser-based full-stack AI app builder from StackBlitz with WebContainers. Iterative.ai is open-source ML ops — DVC and CML for data/model versioning in Git workflows. Pick Bolt.new when the job is apps and you accept low lock-in; pick Iterative.ai when it is engineering-heavy ml teams that want git-based data and experiment workflows and you accept low lock-in. The data we have shows Bolt.new at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Iterative.ai at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Bolt.new is a poor fit for simple projects, and Iterative.ai is a poor fit for data science teams preferring notebook-first uis over git semantics. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Bolt.new is for browser-based full-stack AI app builder from StackBlitz; Iterative.ai is for open-source ML ops — DVC and CML; decide based on ecosystem fit.
verdict_a: Choose Bolt.new if a free tier with paid upgrade from $0 is the right shape and the work maps to browser-based full-stack AI app builder from StackBlitz with WebContainers.
verdict_b: Choose Iterative.ai if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work lines up with open-source ML ops — DVC and CML for data/model versioning in Git workflows.

faq.a: We can't verify it cleanly: Bolt.new starts at $0 (freemium), while Iterative.ai (freemium) doesn't publish an entry price. Request a quote from Iterative.ai before assuming either side is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bolt.new to Iterative.ai?
faq.a: Migration in either direction is relatively cheap — both Bolt.new and Iterative.ai are rated low lock-in, so your configuration and data should port without a rewrite. The realistic cost is team re-training and pipeline QA, not the tools themselves.

Both Bolt.new and Iterative.ai rate the same on developer experience (5/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Iterative.ai a good alternative to Bolt.new?
faq.a: Yes — Iterative.ai is a reasonable alternative to Bolt.new for apps. The practical differences are freemium-vs-freemium billing and low-vs-low lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Iterative.ai as a credible swap.
